turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what appliance can I help you with today?
My Bosch HBL8453UC wall oven won't hold temperature, it started last weekend, and it flashes E110.
Thanks — that's a lot to go on. So your Bosch HBL8453UC wall oven has been losing temperature since last weekend and flashes E110. On this Bosch, E110 is a temperature-sensor circuit fault.
Right, the E110 code — anything I can try before a service call?

Yes — reset the oven by switching it off at the breaker for two minutes, then restore power and set a bake temperature. If E110 comes back on your HBL8453UC, the oven temperature sensor or its wiring is the likely fix and worth a technician.
And you have the model already, right? I don't want to repeat it.
I do — Bosch HBL8453UC, noted with the E110 code from last weekend. No need to repeat it.
Great, I'll try the reset first.
Perfect — if E110 returns after the reset, call back and we'll schedule a technician for the temperature sensor on your Bosch.

symptoms.description: wall oven will not hold temperature and flashes E110

steps_given: Reset the oven by switching it off at the breaker for two minutes, then restore power and set a bake temperature.
If E110 returns, plan to replace the oven temperature sensor or repair its wiring.
